exemplar.title: How does the concentration of potassium chloride (KCl) solution (0 mol/m3, 0.2 mol/m3, 0.4 mol/m3, 0.6 mol/m3, 0.8 mol/m3, 1 mol/m3)  affect the growth of Solanum lycoperscium, measured by stem length increase (cm)?

detailedExemplarData.title: How does the concentration of potassium chloride (KCl) solution (0 mol/m3, 0.2 mol/m3, 0.4 mol/m3, 0.6 mol/m3, 0.8 mol/m3, 1 mol/m3)  affect the growth of Solanum lycoperscium, measured by stem length increase (cm)?

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question framed within a specific tomato agronomic context with clear justification
Methodological considerations identify all key variables and justify replicate number and dosing interval
weaknesses: Procedure lacks full detail on soil mass and pot dimensions, causing minor reproducibility ambiguities
No control for chloride ion effects separate from potassium
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (question context and methodological considerations) meet topâband descriptors by providing specific agronomic rationale and explained controls. The methodology description, while very detailed, has minor omissions (soil mass, pot diameter), placing it in band 5 overall (5â6 descriptor). A score of 5 accurately reflects strong design with small reproducibility gaps.

strengths: Data recording and processing communicated clearly with appropriately labeled tables and graphs
Data processing (means, SD, ANOVA) carried out accurately and is directly relevant to the research question
weaknesses: Consideration of uncertainties is incomplete: heightâmeasurement uncertainty omitted and propagation to final means not fully performed
Uncertainty propagation equations lack clarity and some variable definitions are missing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication and processing of data are strong (band 5), but significant omissions in uncertainty treatment place this criterion at band 3â4. A holistic band 4 score balances these strengths against the gaps in uncertainty consideration.

strengths: Conclusion directly answers the research question and is fully consistent with the processed data
Integration of statistical evidence (ANOVA p-value) supports the identified optimum KCl concentration
weaknesses: Comparison to scientific context is limited to a single study and lacks broader agronomic guideline discussion
Mechanistic claims about chloride effects are not supported by collected data
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is relevant and consistent (band 6) but comparison to the accepted scientific context is only partially developed (band 4). An overall band 4 score reflects a clear, data-consistent conclusion with room for deeper contextual justification.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses are identified with explanations of their relative impact
Proposed improvements are realistic, directly linked to identified limitations, and explanations show how they would enhance validity
weaknesses: Some early improvement suggestions (new experiments) could be more explicitly tied to specific weaknesses
Evaluation could quantify how each improvement would reduce uncertainty or error
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet the highest descriptor for explaining impacts of specific weaknesses and providing explained, relevant improvements (band 6). Minor linking details prevent a top score of 6 under a stringent read, so a score of 5 best reflects the very strong evaluation presented.

strandSelections.reasoning: The conclusion explicitly answers the research question, cites the 0.6 mol mâ»Â³ optimum, explains the polynomial (not linear) relationship and integrates statistical evidence (ANOVA p-value).  Statements align fully with the processed data and acknowledge overlapping error bars, demonstrating consistency.

Raw data tables include units, full concentration labels and five replicates; processed-data tables show averages, SD and propagated uncertainties; graphs carry titles, axes, units, trend line, RÂ² and error bars.  Layout is tidy and values are consistently presented to appropriate precision, demonstrating both clarity and precision.

Processing includes accurate conversion of molarity to mass, calculation of means, standard deviations, polynomial trend fitting and a one-way ANOVA with correct interpretation of F and p values.  No computational errors are evident and the statistical test directly addresses the research question, meeting the âappropriate and accurateâ descriptor.

Specific weaknessesâuneven sunlight, large volumetric uncertainty, limited qualitative observations, low sampling frequency, short durationâare each linked to how they could have influenced growth readings (e.g., lateral growth, water stress).  The student explains the relative impact rather than merely listing them, satisfying the highest descriptor.

The student compares findings to Tariq et al. and discusses physiological mechanisms (PSII inhibition, enzyme activation) using literature citations.  While relevant, the comparison remains brief and centres on a single study; deeper engagement with broader accepted ranges or agronomic guidelines would be needed for a fully âjustifiedâ comparison.

The research question is not only stated but placed within a clear, tomato-specific agronomic context, referencing the nutrient needs of Solanum lycopersicum and a precedent study (Tariq et al., 2019).  This establishes why the range of KCl concentrations is worth investigating and how results could have agricultural value.  The discussion links potassium physiology, toxicity and deficiency directly to the proposed measurement of stem length, demonstrating a specific and appropriate context rather than a broad or generic backdrop.

Instrument uncertainties are listed and one worked propagation example for molarity is shown; error bars represent SD on graphs.  However, height-measurement uncertainty is omitted and propagation to final means is not attempted, leaving notable gaps.  The treatment of uncertainty is therefore present but with significant omissions.

For every identified weakness, a realistic and relevant improvement is proposedâLED lighting, volumetric pipette, daily height recording, 60-day duration, inclusion of leaf colour metricsâaccompanied by an explanation of how each change would strengthen validity or reliability.  This meets the requirement for explained, relevant improvements.

The student identifies independent, dependent and multiple controlled variables, explains how each will be maintained, and justifies choices (e.g., 450 mL watering volume, 10-day dosing interval adopted from literature, five replicates to reduce random error).  Potential confounders such as light and water temperature are recognised, showing an understanding of factors required to obtain relevant and sufficient data.  While depth on sample-size sufficiency could be stronger, the considerations are nevertheless explained rather than merely listed.

A step-by-step procedure, reagent calculations, tables of equipment with uncertainties and photographs of set-up allow another investigator to replicate the work.  Ambiguities remain (soil mass not specified, ambient light/temperature only implicitly stated, pot diameter not given), so reproduction would be possible but with minor clarifications needed.  This fits âreproduced with few ambiguitiesâ rather than the completely unambiguous description expected for the top band.

annotations.comment: The significance level (Î± = 0.05) is implied but not stated in the text when interpreting p and Fâcritâ. Explicitly state Î± and compare p < Î± to support rejection of the null hypothesis.
The conclusion correctly identifies a polynomial rather than linear relationship and the KCl threshold at 0.6 mol mâ»Â³, fully consistent with the data presented.
The discussion compares findings only to Tariq et al. (2019). Broaden the scientific context by referencing additional agronomic guidelines or meta-analyses.
The research question is precisely framed with specific KCl concentrations and a measurable dependent variable, embedding the investigation in an appropriate tomato agronomic context.

The introduction acknowledges that chloride may confound growth effects but no control for chloride alone is included. Consider adding a KâSOâ or KNOâ treatment to isolate potassium-specific responses.
Soil mass is described by depth only. Specify and weigh the soil in grams for each pot to reduce ambiguity and allow full reproducibility of the set-up.
Measuring stem height every three days may miss rapid growth spurts. Increase measurement frequency (e.g., daily) to capture more detailed growth dynamics.
The materials table lists â402.4 g of KCl totalâ which is confusing for multiple concentrations. Instead list grams needed per each concentration to enhance clarity.
Height-measurement tape uncertainty (Â± 0.1 cm) is listed in equipment but not included in propagated uncertainties on processed data. Include this to fully account for all measurement errors.
The uncertainty propagation equations are incorrectly formatted (e.g., â4M/Mâ) and lack clarity in variable definitions. Correct the formula and clearly define each term.
Raw data table columns for replicate measurements are unlabeled, making interpretation difficult. Label each replicate column (e.g., R1âR5) to improve clarity and traceability.
All processed averages use a uniform Â± 0.1 cm uncertainty, disregarding propagated variation on the mean. Calculate and report the propagated uncertainty for each average height.
The criteria for excluding the outlying 1 mol mâ»Â³ data point are not specified. Define a transparent outlier test (e.g., Grubbsâ test) to justify removal.
The ANOVA output table is cluttered and hard to read. Simplify formatting, clearly label p-value in scientific notation and state the Î± threshold explicitly.
Mechanistic claims about chloride effects are plausible but unsupported by collected data. Consider measuring leaf chloride concentration or ascorbate levels in future studies.
Early evaluation suggestions outline new experiments but are not explicitly linked to identified limitations. Tie each proposed improvement to a specific weakness with explained impact.
Strengths section effectively highlights sample size and rapid growth traits, reinforcing reliability. Quantify how five replicates reduced random error for stronger justification.
The weaknesses table clearly links issues to impact and proposes realistic improvements, explaining how each change would increase validity.
Suggest adding a washing step or standardized stirring time in the procedure to ensure complete dissolution of KCl and consistent solution homogeneity.
Planting depth is justified for oxygen but impacts uniformity of emergence. Standardize and record germination time to control variability in initial growth.
Safety considerations omit handling of concentrated KCl solutions. Include guidance on glove use and skin contact prevention when preparing electrolytes.
Ethical considerations note plant pain absence, but omit environmental disposal risks of saline soil. Recommend describing safe disposal procedures to minimize soil salinization.
Bibliography entries vary in format and sometimes omit DOIs. Adopt a consistent citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) including DOIs or URLs for all items.

strands.name: Methodological considerations

strands.name: Methodology description

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is outlined within a broad context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are described.

strands.name: Methodology description
strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced with few ambiguities or omissions.

strands.name: Research question context
strands.content: The research question is described within a specific and appropriate context.

strands.name: Methodological considerations
strands.content: Methodological considerations associated with collecting relevant and sufficient data to answer the research question are explained.

strands.content: The description of the methodology for collecting or selecting data allows for the investigation to be reproduced.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The recording and processing of the data is communicated but is neither clear nor precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows limited evidence of the consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: Some processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with major omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is either clear or precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of a consideration of uncertainties but with some significant omissions or inaccuracies.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out but with some significant omissions, inaccuracies or inconsistencies.

strands.name: Communication of data recording and processing
strands.content: The communication of the recording and processing of the data is both clear and precise.

strands.name: Consideration of uncertainties
strands.content: The recording and processing of data shows evidence of an appropriate consideration of uncertainties.

strands.name: Data processing quality
strands.content: The processing of data relevant to addressing the research question is carried out appropriately and accurately.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is stated that is relevant to the research question but is not supported by the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: The conclusion makes superficial comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is described that is relevant to the research question but is not fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is described that makes some relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Conclusion relevance and consistency
strands.content: A conclusion is justified that is relevant to the research question and fully consistent with the analysis presented.

strands.name: Scientific context comparison
strands.content: A conclusion is justified through relevant comparison to the accepted scientific context.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: No work presented or work does not reach the standard described.

strands.name: Improvements

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report states generic methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation are stated.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report describes specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are described.

strands.name: Methodological weaknesses
strands.content: The report explains the relative impact of specific methodological weaknesses or limitations.

strands.name: Improvements
strands.content: Realistic improvements to the investigation, that are relevant to the identified weaknesses or limitations, are explained.
